AP Candidate Decision.description: a 5 String Based Enum Datapoint where the Logic and Value shall be ['Roaming Initiated', 'Link switch Initiated', 'Connection Initiated', 'no changes'] : If the AP selection starts roaming 'AP candidate decision' is 'Roaming Initiated' If the AP selection starts link switch 'AP candidate decision' is 'Link switch Initiated' If the AP selection starts connection 'AP candidate decision' is 'Connection Initiated' If the AP selection trigger disconnection 'AP candidate decision' is 'Disconnect Initiated' otherwise 'no changes'

STA MAC Address.description: The MAC Address Identifier of the Local Wireless product. Typically representing the Self-MAC-Address (may be created dynamically, and the one that is actually used). Structured with 6 Octets (Bytes) values. e.g. 12:34:56:78:9A:BC

AP MLD MAC Address.description: The AP side Multi-Link-Device, AP MLD MAC Address

Is MLD AP.description: Indicates whether reported AP is a Multi-Link-Device (MLD, WiFi7) AP

Connection Type.description: The type of the reported Connection (mainly used for Dual - BSS / Dual - Radio mode cases, and for the future 802.11be Multi - Link operations). Options: [Primary BSS, Secondary BSS, Primary MLD BSS, Secondary MLD BSS] Default : Primary BSS.

Connection Link ID.description: Identify the Links ID (for Multi-Links-Device, WiFi7 Case), Indicates the Link ID that is reported by MLD AP. In case of Legacy Mode (Non-multi-Links, i.e. Single-Link-Device), set to ‘0’.

BSSID.description: The BSSID of the AP as defined by 802.11 spec. In case of Multi-link AP (802.11be), it is the BSSID (i.e. the MAC Address) of a specific link within the MLD. Note: Should not be confused with the MLD MAC Address, which is shared by all links in MLD and is reported via a separate and dedicated Data-Point.

Channel.description: The Current Channel used by this Network (AP)

Band.description: The Current Band used by this Network (AP)

Operational Channel Width.description: The actual operational used Channel Width over the active link

Radio Type.description: In case DP is reported with 'connection' context - The agreed Wi-Fi generation as part of the association process, based on the the Legacy/HT/VHT/HE/EHT/UHR capabilities that are reported in AP's Association-Response. However, in case the report is following connection failure prior to association completion, the report will be based on the Max - Common Wi-Fi Generation between our Device and AP (as reported for 'scan' context). In case DP is reported with 'scan' context - Max (most advanced) supported Wi-Fi generation of the AP, based on the the Legacy / HT / VHT / HE / EHT / UHR capabilities that are reported in AP's Beacon/Probe-Response. The following is the Spec - Name vs Wi-Fi Generations Mapping : Legacy - Wi-Fi3 HT - Wi-Fi4 VHT - Wi-Fi5 HE - Wi-Fi6 EHT - Wi-Fi7, UHR - Wi-Fi8

Latest Effective Channel Utilization.description: Latest Effective Channel Utilization : for Non associated APs, this Value shall be the Latest Channel Utilization Value from the Scan Beacon (if exists), for the associated AP the we need to subtract the Channel Load By Us value from the AP Channel Utilization The substruction of the Client Channel Utilization (Self) from the AP Total reported Utilization

Latest Station Count.description: Latest Station Count as reported by the AP

BSSID.description: The BSSID of the AP as defined by 802.11 spec. In case of Multi-link AP (802.11be), it is the BSSID (i.e. the MAC Address) of a specific link within the MLD. Note: Should not be confused with the MLD MAC Address, which is shared by all links in MLD and is reported via a separate and dedicated Data-Point.

AP MLD MAC Address.description: The AP side Multi-Link-Device, AP MLD MAC Address

Is MLD AP.description: Indicates whether reported AP is a Multi-Link-Device (MLD, WiFi7) AP

Channel.description: The Current Channel used by this Network (AP)

Band.description: The Current Band used by this Network (AP)

Operational Channel Width.description: The actual operational used Channel Width over the active link

Radio Type.description: In case DP is reported with 'connection' context - The agreed Wi-Fi generation as part of the association process, based on the the Legacy/HT/VHT/HE/EHT/UHR capabilities that are reported in AP's Association-Response. However, in case the report is following connection failure prior to association completion, the report will be based on the Max - Common Wi-Fi Generation between our Device and AP(as reported for 'scan' context). In case DP is reported with 'scan' context - Max(most advanced) supported Wi-Fi generation of the AP, based on the the Legacy / HT / VHT / HE / EHT / UHR capabilities that are reported in AP's Beacon/Probe-Response. The following is the Spec - Name vs Wi-Fi Generations Mapping : Legacy - Wi-Fi3 HT - Wi-Fi4 VHT - Wi-Fi5 HE - Wi-Fi6 EHT - Wi-Fi7, UHR - Wi-Fi8

Latest Effective Channel Utilization.description: Latest Effective Channel Utilization : for Non associated APs, this Value shall be the Latest Channel Utilization Value from the Scan Beacon(if exists), for the associated AP the we need to subtract the Channel Load By Us value from the AP Channel Utilization The substruction of the Client Channel Utilization(Self) from the AP Total reported Utilization

Latest Station Count.description: Latest Station Count as reported by the AP

Is Associated.description: The candidate list contains list of APs the device want to Roam or Connect to and the AP it is associated with. The Candidate Is Associated is True for the associated AP and False for all the other roaming candidates.

AP Grade.description: AP grade as defined by the AP selection algorithm

oneOf.description: Due to bad coverage of APs in the area, client is connected with poor RSSI for a long time (Low-RSSI Insight Time Threshold) without roaming candidates

AP MLD MAC Address.description: The AP side Multi-Link-Device, AP MLD MAC Address

STA MAC Address.description: The MAC Address Identifier of the Local Wireless product. Typically representing the Self-MAC-Address (may be created dynamically, and the one that is actually used). Structured with 6 Octets (Bytes) values. e.g. 12:34:56:78:9A:BC

SSID.description: The current Wi-Fi SSID (Network Name) the Device is connected to

Authentication Environment Type.description: Environment type (Personal or Enterprise) as deduced from the authentication method

STA MAC Address.description: The MAC Address Identifier of the Local Wireless product. Typically representing the Self-MAC-Address (may be created dynamically, and the one that is actually used). Structured with 6 Octets (Bytes) values. e.g. 12:34:56:78:9A:BC

Is MLD AP.description: Indicates whether reported AP is a Multi-Link-Device (MLD, WiFi7) AP

AP MLD MAC Address.description: The AP side Multi-Link-Device, AP MLD MAC Address

Connection Type.description: The type of the reported Connection (mainly used for Dual - BSS / Dual - Radio mode cases, and for the future 802.11be Multi - Link operations). Options: [Primary BSS, Secondary BSS, Primary MLD BSS, Secondary MLD BSS] Default : Primary BSS.

SSID.description: The Current Wi-Fi SSID (Network Name) The Device Is Connected To

AP Manufacturer Name.description: The Name of the AP Manufacturer: e.g., Netgear

AP Model Name.description: The Name of the AP Model: e.g., R7000

Windows OS Link Quality.description: The Wi-Fi Signal Strength Indication from the OS Level

Connection Link ID.description: Identify the Links ID (for Multi-Links-Device, WiFi7 Case), Indicates the Link ID that is reported by MLD AP. In case of Legacy Mode (Non-multi-Links, i.e. Single-Link-Device), set to '0'.

BSSID.description: The BSSID of the AP as defined by 802.11 spec. In case of Multi-link AP (802.11be), it is the BSSID (i.e. the MAC Address) of a specific link within the MLD. Note: Should not be confused with the MLD MAC Address, which is shared by all links in MLD and is reported via a separate and dedicated Data-Point.

Spatial Streams Tx.description: Max number of Spatial Streams that are mutually supported by AP and Client on Tx

Band.description: The Current Band used by this Network (AP)

Channel.description: The Current Channel used by this Network (AP)

Operational Channel Width.description: The actual operational used Channel Width over the active link

Previous Channel.description: The Previous Channel used by this Network (the one the AP Moved from)

Beacon Frame.description: The latest raw Beacon of reported AP

SSID.description: The current Wi-Fi SSID (Network Name) the Device is connected to

BSSID.description: The BSSID of the AP as defined by 802.11 spec. In case of Multi-link AP (802.11be), it is the BSSID (i.e. the MAC Address) of a specific link within the MLD. Band.description: The Current Band used by this Network (AP)

Channel.description: The Current Channel used by this Network (AP)

Channel Width.description: The actual operational used Channel Width over the active link

Conflicting SSID.description: The SSID String, from Scan List, of the conflicting AP-channel (Non-Orthogonal channel) with Current Channel

Conflicting BSSID.description: The BSSID (MAC Address), from Scan List, of the conflicting AP-Channel (Non-Orthogonal channel) with Current Channel

Conflicting Channel.description: The Channel Number, from Scan List, of the Conflicting AP-Channel (Non-Orthogonal channel) with Current Channel

Conflicting Channel Width.description: The Channel Width Value, from Scan List, of the conflicting channel (Non-Orthogonal channel) with Current Channel

Time Of Frame Reception.description:
Timestamp of the Discovery Frame (Beacon or Probe-Response) referring to reported AP/Link.
Notice: If during the same Scan operation, both Beacon and Probe-Response are received for same AP, the latest received frame (i.e. either the Beacon or the Probe-Response) shall be stored by Driver and used for the AP discovery process. This DP refers to the reception time of the Beacon or Probe-Response (whichever was received last) that was stored by Driver and used for the AP discovery process.

Radio Type.description: Actual Radio Type (Technology) e.g., Legacy/HT/VHT/HE/EHT

Bin for every 1C over the range of 106C to 124C. Bin for the range of 125C to 140C

Periodic RSSI A Beacon Histogram.description: Antenna A, Beacon Frame based, Short Period RSSI. Values are defined: 0 to -120 dBm Histogram bin value contains a counter of number of packets within the bin range. There will be a histogram for each BSSID (or MLD) of the connection it relates to. There will be a histogram for each chain. Non-Valid results or antenna not used (e.g. the packet had been received by one chain, then the data of the non-active chain shall not be included and it is marked with RSSI=0) shall be converted to 0.   Histogram of 36 bins: Bin for the range of -120dBm to -95dBm. Bin for every 1dB over the range of -94dBm to -75dBm. Bin for every 2dB over the range of -74dBm to -65dBm. Bin for every 4dB over the range of -64dBm to -45dBm. Bin for every 8dB over the range of -44dBm to -21dBm. Bin for the range of -20dBm to -1dBm. Bin for the value of 0 is for packets not received in this chain

Periodic RSSI A Data Histogram.description: Antenna A, Data Frame based, Short Period RSSI. Values are defined: 0 to -120 dBm Histogram bin value contains a counter of number of packets within the bin range. There will be a histogram for each BSSID (or MLD) of the connection it relates to. There will be a histogram for each chain. Non-Valid results or antenna not used (e.g. the packet had been received by one chain, then the data of the non-active chain shall not be included and it is marked with RSSI=0) shall be converted to 0.   Histogram of 36 bins: Bin for the range of -120dBm to -95dBm. Bin for every 1dB over the range of -94dBm to -75dBm. Bin for every 2dB over the range of -74dBm to -65dBm. Bin for every 4dB over the range of -64dBm to -45dBm. Bin for every 8dB over the range of -44dBm to -21dBm. Bin for the range of -20dBm to -1dBm. Bin for the value of 0 is for packets not received in this chain

Periodic BSSLoad IE Station Number Histo.description: Based on Beacon and Probe Response BSS Load IE for the specific BSS connection received by the driver during short measurement periods; Histogram bin (6 bins) range: [1..5, 6..10, 11..15, 16..20, 21..30, Greater than 30]

Periodic BSSLoad IE Channel Utilization.description: Based on Beacon and Probe-Response BSS Load IE for the specific BSS connection received by the driver during short measurement periods; Histogram bin range: 0 - 255; Histogram bin : 16 bins with a range step 16

Periodic BSSLoad IE Available Admission.description: Based on Beacon and Probe-Response BSS Load IE for the specific BSS connection received by the driver during short measurement periods; Histogram bin range : 0 - 65535; Histogram bin : 16 bins with a range step 4096

Periodic CCA Indications During Tx in Li.description: Number of CCA indicated when trying to transmit during the last Short Period

Periodic Tx Attempts in Link Counter.description: Number of Tx Attempts (not Tx due CCA) during the last Short Period

Periodic Tx Retries in Link Counter.description: Number of MPDUs retransmissions during the last short period

Periodic Tx Packet-Loss in Link Counter.description: Number of dropped MPDUs which were not successfully transmitted during the last Short Period

Periodic Tx MPDUs per MCS Data Rate in L.description: Transmitted MPDUs per MCS/BW/GI/NSS during last Short Period. Histogram of 480 bins, according to the following keys: BW (20MHz, 40MHz, 80MHz, 160MHz, 320MHz) MCS (0-15) NSS (1ss,2ss) GI (1.6usec, 0.8usec, 3.2usec)

Periodic Tx MPDUs per OFDM Rate in Link.description: Transmitted MPDUs per OFDM Rate during the last short period. Histogram of 8 bins: 1 per rate [6,9,12,18,24,36,48,54]

Periodic Tx MPDUs per CCK Rate in Link H.description: Transmitted MPDUs per CCK Rate during the last Short Period. Histogram of 4 Bins: 1 per rate [1,2,5.5,11]

Periodic Failed Tx MPDUs per MCS Data Ra.description: MPDUs transmission failures per MCS/BW/GI/NSS during last Short Period. Histogram of 480 bins: according to the following keys: BW (20MHz, 40MHz, 80MHz, 160MHz, 320MHz) MCS (0-15) NSS (1ss, 2ss) GI (1.6usec, 0.8usec, 3.2usec)

Periodic Failed Tx MPDUs per OFDM Rate i.description: MPDUs transmission failures per OFDM Rate during the last Short Period. Histogram of 8 bins: 1 per rate [6,9,12,18,24,36,48,54]

Periodic Failed Tx MPDUs per CCK Rate in.description: MPDUs transmission failures per CCK Rate during last Short Period. Histogram of 4 Bins: 1 per rate [1,2,5.5,11]

Periodic Rx MPDUs per MCS Data Rate in L.description: Received MPDUs per MCS/BW/GI/NSS during last Short Period. Histogram of 480 bins, according to the following keys: BW (20MHz, 40MHz, 80MHz, 160MHz, 320MHz) MCS (0 - 15) NSS (1ss, 2ss) GI (1.6usec, 0.8usec, 3.2usec)

Periodic Rx MPDUs per OFDM Rate in Link.description: Received MPDUs per OFDM Rate during last Short Period. Histogram of 8 Bins: 1 per rate [6,9,12,18,24,36,48,54]

Periodic Rx MPDUs per CCK Rate in Link H.description: Received MPDUs per CCK Rate during last Short Period. Histogram of 4 bins: 1 per rate [1,2,5.5,11]

Periodic Tx MPDUs Per TID in Link Histog.description: Transmitted Data MPDUs per TID during the last Short Period. Histogram of 8 bins: 1 for each TID[0, 1, ..., 7]

Periodic Rx MPDUs Per TID in Link Histog.description: Received Data MPDUs per TID during the last Short Period. Histogram of 8 bins: 1 for each TID[0, 1, ..., 7]

Periodic Bad-CRCs in Link Counter.description: Number of received MPDUs with CRC error during the last Short Period

Periodic Missed Beacons Histogram.description: Histogram of all Missed beacons with the following 4 Bins: 1 and above Missed Beacons, 3 and above Missed Beacons, 5 and above Missed Beacons, 7 and above Missed Beacons

Periodic RSSI A Silence Histogram.description: Antenna A measures of each frame of the short period RSSI Between Tx-Rx at 'silence' time - to represent the Noise at the antenna port when no WiFi traffic is expected Values are defined: 0 to -120 dBm Histogram bin value contains a counter of number of packets within the bin range. There will be a histogram for each BSSID (or MLD) of the connection it relates to. There will be a histogram for each chain. Non-Valid results or antenna not used (e.g. the packet had been received by one chain, then the data of the non-active chain shall not be included and it is marked with RSSI=0) shall be converted to 0.   Histogram of 36 bins: Bin for the range of -120dBm to -95dBm. Bin for every 1dB over the range of -94dBm to -75dBm. Bin for every 2dB over the range of -74dBm to -65dBm. Bin for every 4dB over the range of -64dBm to -45dBm. Bin for every 8dB over the range of -44dBm to -21dBm. Bin for the range of -20dBm to -1dBm. Bin for the value of 0 is for packets not received in this chain

Periodic RSSI B Silence Histogram.description: Antenna B measures of each frame of the short period RSSI Between Tx-Rx at 'silence' time - to represent the Noise at the antenna port when no WiFi traffic is expected Values are defined: 0 to -120 dBm Histogram bin value contains a counter of number of packets within the bin range. There will be a histogram for each BSSID (or MLD) of the connection it relates to. There will be a histogram for each chain. Non-Valid results or antenna not used (e.g. the packet had been received by one chain, then the data of the non-active chain shall not be included and it is marked with RSSI=0) shall be converted to 0.   Histogram of 36 bins: Bin for the range of -120dBm to -95dBm. Bin for every 1dB over the range of -94dBm to -75dBm. Bin for every 2dB over the range of -74dBm to -65dBm. Bin for every 4dB over the range of -64dBm to -45dBm. Bin for every 8dB over the range of -44dBm to -21dBm. Bin for the range of -20dBm to -1dBm. Bin for the value of 0 is for packets not received in this chain

Periodic Frequency Errors in Link Histog.description: Frequency Error Short Period Histogram. Histogram bin value contains a counter of number of packets within the bin range. There will be a histogram for each BSSID (or MLD) of the connection it relates to Using absolute values of the Freq Error in [kHz] units Histogram of 24 bins: Bin for every 2kHz over the range of 0Hz to 9kHz. Bin for every 4kHz over the range of 10kHz to 29kHz. Bin for every 8kHz over the range of 30kHz to 69kHz. Bin for every 16kHz over the range of 70kHz to 197kHz. Bin for the range of 198kHz to 1000kHz

Periodic SNR A Histogram.description: Antenna A SNR Short Period histogram Histogram of 41 bins: Bin for the range of -20dB to 0dB. Bin for every 1dB over the range of 1dB to 39dB. Bin for the range of 40dB to 60dB

Periodic SNR B Histogram.description: Antenna B SNR Short Period histogram Histogram of 41 bins: Bin for the range of -20dB to 0dB. Bin for every 1dB over the range of 1dB to 39dB. Bin for the range of 40dB to 60dB

Periodic STA Self Channel Utilization Hi.description: STA load for Short Period. Histogram bin range: 0 - 255 Histogram bin: 16 bins with a range step 16

Periodic Tx MPDUs in AMPDUs in Link Hist.description: Accumulate counting of Tx Packets (MPDUs) in AMPDUs from start of Short Period connection per Link, Histogram: start from 0- 15 MPDUs, 16-31 MPDUs Jumps of 16, to 992-1007 MPDUs, 1008-1023 MPDUs

Periodic Full BA Tx MPDUs in AMPDUs in L.description: Accumulate counting of Full BA Tx Packets (MPDUs) in AMPDUs from start of Short Period connection per Link, Histogram: 64 MPDUs, 256 MPDUs, 512 MPDUs, 1024 MPDUs

Periodic Rx MPDUs in AMPDUs in Link Hist.description: Accumulate counting of Rx Packets (MPDUs) in AMPDUs from start of Short Period connection per Link, Histogram: start from 0- 15 MPDUs, 16-31 MPDUs Jumps of 16, to 992-1007 MPDUs, 1008-1023 MPDUs

Periodic Full BA Rx MPDUs in AMPDUs in L.description: Accumulate counting of Full BA Rx Packets (MPDUs) in AMPDUs from start of Short Period connection, Histogram: 64 MPDUs, 256 MPDUs, 512 MPDUs, 1024 MPDUs

Periodic Tx RU Size in Link Histogram.description: Transmitted PPDU RU Size per Link during last Short Period. Histogram of 8 bins: 26 Tones, 52 Tones, 106 Tones, 242 Tones, 484 Tones, 996 Tones, 996x2 Tones, 996x4 Tones (the units are Strings representing number of Tones)

Periodic Average frame Latency per TID i.description: Average Transmitted Data Frames Latency per TID per MAC in specific Link in Short Period. Histogram of 8 bins: 1 for each TID[0, 1, ..., 7]

Periodic Max Data Frames Latency per TID.description: Max Transmitted Data Frames Latency per TID per Link in Short Period. Histogram of 8 bins: 1 for each TID[0, 1, ..., 7]. Unit: microseconds

Periodic Air Time in Link Sum.description: Total Rx and Tx Air Time in Short Periods per Link. Unit: microseconds

Periodic Tx Air Time in Link Counter.description: Total Tx Only Air Time in Short Periods per Link. Unit: microseconds

Periodic Rx Air Time in Link Counter.description: Total Rx Only Air Time in Short Periods per Link. Unit: microseconds

Periodic Channel Load.description: Channel Load in Link indicates the channel load percentage (Unknown can be a Value as well with Value of 255). Notes: Conditions for customer for ignoring Channel Load Results : If ELP > ACT, In Wi-Fi-7 EMLSR mode, Channel Load is not updated.

Periodic Channel Load by US.description: Channel Load by Us in Link indicates the channel load percentage of the STA. Note: Conditions for customer for ignoring Channel Load Results: If ELP > ACT In Wi-Fi - 7 EMLSR mode the channel Load Histogram is not updated

Periodic Channel Load Active Total Time.description: Channel Load Active Total Time in Link in microseconds. In Wi-Fi7 EMLSR mode, ACT is set to 0. Unit: microseconds

Periodic Channel Load Elapsed Time (ELP).description: Channel Load Elapsed Time (ELP) Without Measurements in Link in microseconds. In Wi-Fi7 EMLSR mode, ELP is set to 0. Unit: microseconds

Values are defined: 0 to -120 dBm Histogram bin value contains a counter of number of packets within the bin range. There will be a histogram for each BSSID (or MLD) of the connection it relates to. There will be a histogram for each chain. Non-Valid results or antenna not used (e.g. the packet had been received by one chain, then the data of the non-active chain shall not be included and it is marked with RSSI=0) shall be converted to 0.   Histogram of 36 bins: Bin for the range of -120dBm to -95dBm. Bin for every 1dB over the range of -94dBm to -75dBm. Bin for every 2dB over the range of -74dBm to -65dBm. Bin for every 4dB over the range of -64dBm to -45dBm. Bin for every 8dB over the range of -44dBm to -21dBm. Bin for the range of -20dBm to -1dBm. Bin for the value of 0 is for packets not received in this chain

Periodic RSSI B Data Histogram.description: Antenna B, Data Frame based, Short Period RSSI. Values are defined: 0 to -120 dBm Histogram bin value contains a counter of number of packets within the bin range. There will be a histogram for each BSSID (or MLD) of the connection it relates to. There will be a histogram for each chain. Non-Valid results or antenna not used (e.g. the packet had been received by one chain, then the data of the non-active chain shall not be included and it is marked with RSSI=0) shall be converted to 0.   Histogram of 36 bins: Bin for the range of -120dBm to -95dBm. Bin for every 1dB over the range of -94dBm to -75dBm. Bin for every 2dB over the range of -74dBm to -65dBm. Bin for every 4dB over the range of -64dBm to -45dBm. Bin for every 8dB over the range of -44dBm to -21dBm. Bin for the range of -20dBm to -1dBm. Periodic BSSLoad IE Station Number Histo.description: Based on Beacon and Probe Response BSS Load IE for the specific BSS connection received by the driver during short measurement periods; Histogram bin (6 bins) range: [1..5, 6..10, 11..15, 16..20, 21..30, Greater than 30]

Periodic BSSLoad IE Channel Utilization.description: Based on Beacon and Probe-Response BSS Load IE for the specific BSS connection received by the driver during short measurement periods; Histogram bin range: 0 - 255; Histogram bin : 16 bins with a range step 16

Periodic BSSLoad IE Available Admission.description: Based on Beacon and Probe-Response BSS Load IE for the specific BSS connection received by the driver during short measurement periods; Histogram bin range : 0 - 65535; Histogram bin : 16 bins with a range step 4096

Periodic CCA Indications During Tx in Li.description: Number of CCA indicated when trying to transmit during the last Short Period

Periodic Tx Attempts in Link Counter.description: Number of Tx Attempts (not Tx due CCA) during the last Short Period

Periodic Tx Retries in Link Counter.description: Number of MPDUs retransmissions during the last short period

Periodic Tx Packet-Loss in Link Counter.description: Number of dropped MPDUs which were not successfully transmitted during the last Short Period

Periodic Tx MPDUs per MCS Data Rate in L.description: Transmitted MPDUs per MCS/BW/GI/NSS during last Short Period. Histogram of 480 bins, according to the following keys: BW (20MHz, 40MHz, 80MHz, 160MHz, 320MHz) MCS (0-15) NSS (1ss,2ss) GI (1.6usec, 0.8usec, 3.2usec)

Periodic Tx MPDUs per OFDM Rate in Link.description: Transmitted MPDUs per OFDM Rate during the last short period. Histogram of 8 bins: 1 per rate [6,9,12,18,24,36,48,54]

Periodic Tx MPDUs per CCK Rate in Link H.description: Transmitted MPDUs per CCK Rate during the last Short Period. Histogram of 4 Bins: 1 per rate [1,2,5.5,11]

Periodic Failed Tx MPDUs per MCS Data Ra.description: MPDUs transmission failures per MCS/BW/GI/NSS during last Short Period. Histogram of 480 bins: according to the following keys: BW (20MHz, 40MHz, 80MHz, 160MHz, 320MHz) MCS (0-15) NSS (1ss, 2ss) GI (1.6usec, 0.8usec, 3.2usec)

Periodic Failed Tx MPDUs per OFDM Rate i.description: MPDUs transmission failures per OFDM Rate during the last Short Period. Histogram of 8 bins: 1 per rate [6,9,12,18,24,36,48,54]

Periodic Failed Tx MPDUs per CCK Rate in.description: MPDUs transmission failures per CCK Rate during last Short Period. Histogram of 4 Bins: 1 per rate [1,2,5.5,11]

Periodic Rx MPDUs per MCS Data Rate in L.description: Received MPDUs per MCS/BW/GI/NSS during last Short Period. Histogram of 480 bins, according to the following keys: BW (20MHz, 40MHz, 80MHz, 160MHz, 320MHz) MCS (0 - 15) NSS (1ss, 2ss) GI (1.6usec, 0.8usec, 3.2usec)

Periodic Rx MPDUs per OFDM Rate in Link.description: Received MPDUs per OFDM Rate during last Short Period. Histogram of 8 Bins: 1 per rate [6,9,12,18,24,36,48,54]

Periodic Rx MPDUs per CCK Rate in Link H.description: Received MPDUs per CCK Rate during last Short Period. Histogram of 4 bins: 1 per rate [1,2,5.5,11]

Periodic Tx MPDUs Per TID in Link Histog.description: Transmitted Data MPDUs per TID during the last Short Period. Histogram of 8 bins: 1 for each TID[0, 1, ..., 7]

Periodic Rx MPDUs Per TID in Link Histog.description: Received Data MPDUs per TID during the last Short Period. Histogram of 8 bins: 1 for each TID[0, 1, ..., 7]

Periodic Bad-CRCs in Link Counter.description: Number of received MPDUs with CRC error during the last Short Period

Bin for every 4dB over the range of -64dBm to -45dBm. Bin for every 8dB over the range of -44dBm to -21dBm. Bin for the range of -20dBm to -1dBm. Bin for the value of 0 is for packets not received in this chain

Periodic Frequency Errors in Link Histog.description: Frequency Error Short Period Histogram. Histogram bin value contains a counter of number of packets within the bin range. There will be a histogram for each BSSID (or MLD) of the connection it relates to Using absolute values of the Freq Error in [kHz] units Histogram of 24 bins: Bin for every 2kHz over the range of 0Hz to 9kHz. Bin for every 4kHz over the range of 10kHz to 29kHz. Bin for every 8kHz over the range of 30kHz to 69kHz. Bin for every 16kHz over the range of 70kHz to 197kHz. Bin for the range of 198kHz to 1000kHz

Periodic SNR A Histogram.description: Antenna A SNR Short Period histogram Histogram of 41 bins: Bin for the range of -20dB to 0dB. Bin for every 1dB over the range of 1dB to 39dB. Bin for the range of 40dB to 60dB

Periodic SNR B Histogram.description: Antenna B SNR Short Period histogram Histogram of 41 bins: Bin for the range of -20dB to 0dB. Bin for every 1dB over the range of 1dB to 39dB. Bin for the range of 40dB to 60dB

Periodic STA Self Channel Utilization Hi.description: STA load for Short Period. Histogram bin range: 0 - 255 Histogram bin: 16 bins with a range step 16

Periodic Tx MPDUs in AMPDUs in Link Hist.description: Accumulate counting of Tx Packets (MPDUs) in AMPDUs from start of Short Period connection per Link, Histogram: start from 0- 15 MPDUs, 16-31 MPDUs Jumps of 16, to 992-1007 MPDUs, 1008-1023 MPDUs

Periodic Full BA Tx MPDUs in AMPDUs in L.description: Accumulate counting of Full BA Tx Packets (MPDUs) in AMPDUs from start of Short Period connection per Link, Histogram: 64 MPDUs, 256 MPDUs, 512 MPDUs, 1024 MPDUs

Periodic Rx MPDUs in AMPDUs in Link Hist.description: Accumulate counting of Rx Packets (MPDUs) in AMPDUs from start of Short Period connection per Link, Histogram: start from 0- 15 MPDUs, 16-31 MPDUs Jumps of 16, to 992-1007 MPDUs, 1008-1023 MPDUs

Periodic Full BA Rx MPDUs in AMPDUs in L.description: Accumulate counting of Full BA Rx Packets (MPDUs) in AMPDUs from start of Short Period connection, Histogram: 64 MPDUs, 256 MPDUs, 512 MPDUs, 1024 MPDUs

Periodic Tx RU Size in Link Histogram.description: Transmitted PPDU RU Size per Link during last Short Period. Histogram of 8 bins: 26 Tones, 52 Tones, 106 Tones, 242 Tones, 484 Tones, 996 Tones, 996x2 Tones, 996x4 Tones (the units are Strings representing number of Tones)

Periodic Average frame Latency per TID i.description: Average Transmitted Data Frames Latency per TID per MAC in specific Link in Short Period. Histogram of 8 bins: 1 for each TID[0, 1, ..., 7]

Periodic Max Data Frames Latency per TID.description: Max Transmitted Data Frames Latency per TID per Link in Short Period. Histogram of 8 bins: 1 for each TID[0, 1, ..., 7]. Unit: microseconds

Periodic Air Time in Link Sum.description: Total Rx and Tx Air Time in Short Periods per Link. Unit: microseconds

Periodic Tx Air Time in Link Counter.description: Total Tx Only Air Time in Short Periods per Link. Unit: microseconds

Periodic Rx Air Time in Link Counter.description: Total Rx Only Air Time in Short Periods per Link. Unit: microseconds

Periodic Channel Load.description: Channel Load in Link indicates the channel load percentage (Unknown can be a Value as well with Value of 255). Notes: Conditions for customer for ignoring Channel Load Results : If ELP > ACT, In Wi-Fi-7 EMLSR mode, Channel Load is not updated.

Periodic Channel Load by US.description: Channel Load by Us in Link indicates the channel load percentage of the STA. Note: Conditions for customer for ignoring Channel Load Results: If ELP > ACT In Wi-Fi - 7 EMLSR mode the channel Load Histogram is not updated

Periodic Channel Load Active Total Time.description: Channel Load Active Total Time in Link in microseconds. In Wi-Fi7 EMLSR mode, ACT is set to 0. Unit: microseconds

Periodic Channel Load Elapsed Time (ELP).description: Channel Load Elapsed Time (ELP) Without Measurements in Link in microseconds. In Wi-Fi7 EMLSR mode, ELP is set to 0. Unit: microseconds

Connection Link ID.description: Identify the Links ID (for Multi-Links-Device, WiFi7 Case), Indicates the Link ID that is reported by MLD AP. In case of Legacy Mode (Non-multi-Links, i.e. Single-Link-Device), set to '0'.

BSSID.description: The BSSID of the AP as defined by 802.11 spec. In case of Multi-link AP (802.11be), it is the BSSID (i.e. the MAC Address) of a specific link within the MLD. Note: Should not be confused with the MLD MAC Address, which is shared by all links in MLD and is reported via a separate and dedicated Data-Point.

Band.description: The Current Band used by this Network (AP)

Channel.description: The Current Channel used by this Network (AP)

Rx MPDUs per MCS Data Rate in Link Histo.description: Received MPDUs per MCS / BW / GI / NSS from start of current connection. Histogram of 480 bins, according to the following keys: BW (20MHz, 40MHz, 80MHz, 160MHz, 320MHz) MCS (0 - 15) NSS(1ss, 2ss) GI (1.6usec, 0.8usec, 3.2usec)

Rx MPDUs per OFDM Rate in Link Histogram.description: Received MPDUs per OFDM Rate from start of current connection. Histogram of 8 bins: 1 per rate [6,9,12,18,24,36,48,54]

Rx MPDUs per CCK Rate in Link Histogram.description: Received MPDUs per CCK Rate from start of current connection. Histogram of 4 bins: 1 per rate [1,2,5.5,11]

Tx MPDUs Per TID in Link Histogram.description: Transmitted Data MPDUs per TID from the start of the current connection. Histogram of 8 bins: 1 for each TID[0, 1, ..., 7]

Rx MPDUs Per TID in Link Histogram.description: Received Data MPDUs per TID from the start of the current connection. Histogram of 8 bins: 1 for each TID[0, 1, ..., 7]

Bad-CRCs in Link Counter.description: Number of received MPDUs with CRC error from start of current connection

Missed Beacons Histogram.description: Histogram of all Missed beacons with the following 4 Bins: 1 and above Missed Beacons, 3 and above Missed Beacons, 5 and above Missed Beacons, 7 and above Missed Beacons

RSSI A Silence Histogram.description: Antenna A measures of each frames of the RSSI Between Tx-Rx at 'silence' time - to represent the Noise at the antenna port when no WiFi traffic is expected Values are defined: 0 to -120 dBm Histogram bin value contains a counter of number of packets within the bin range. There will be a histogram for each BSSID (or MLD) of the connection it relates to. There will be a histogram for each chain. Non-Valid results or antenna not used (e.g. the packet had been received by one chain, then the data of the non-active chain shall not be included and it is marked with RSSI=0) shall be converted to 0.   Histogram of 36 bins: Bin for the range of -120dBm to -95dBm. Bin for every 1dB over the range of -94dBm to -75dBm. Bin for every 2dB over the range of -74dBm to -65dBm. Bin for every 4dB over the range of -64dBm to -45dBm. Bin for every 8dB over the range of -44dBm to -21dBm. Bin for the range of -20dBm to -1dBm. Bin for the value of 0 is for packets not received in this chain

RSSI B Silence Histogram.description: Antenna B measures of each frame of the RSSI Between Tx-Rx at 'silence' time - to represent the Noise at the antenna port when no WiFi traffic is expected Values are defined: 0 to -120 dBm Histogram bin value contains a counter of number of packets within the bin range. There will be a histogram for each BSSID (or MLD) of the connection it relates to. There will be a histogram for each chain. Non-Valid results or antenna not used (e.g. the packet had been received by one chain, then the data of the non-active chain shall not be included and it is marked with RSSI=0) shall be converted to 0.   Histogram of 36 bins: Bin for the range of -120dBm to -95dBm. Bin for every 1dB over the range of -94dBm to -75dBm. Bin for every 2dB over the range of -74dBm to -65dBm. Bin for every 4dB over the range of -64dBm to -45dBm. Bin for every 8dB over the range of -44dBm to -21dBm. Bin for the range of -20dBm to -1dBm. Bin for the value of 0 is for packets not received in this chain

Frequency Errors in Link Histogram.description: Frequency Error Histogram Histogram bin value contains a counter of number of packets within the bin range There will be a histogram for each BSSID (or MLD) of the connection it relates to Using absolute values of the Freq Error in [kHz] units Histogram of 24 bins: Bin for every 2kHz over the range of 0Hz to 9kHz. Bin for every 4kHz over the range of 10kHz to 29kHz. Bin for every 8kHz over the range of 30kHz to 69kHz. Bin for every 16kHz over the range of 70kHz to 197kHz. Bin for the range of 198kHz to 1000kHz

SNR A Histogram.description: Antenna A SNR Values histogram Histogram of 41 bins: Bin for the range of -20dB to 0dB. Bin for every 1dB over the range of 1dB to 39dB. Bin for the range of 40dB to 60dB

SNR B Histogram.description: Antenna B SNR Values histogram Histogram of 41 bins: Bin for the range of -20dB to 0dB. Bin for every 1dB over the range of 1dB to 39dB. Bin for the range of 40dB to 60dB

STA Self Channel Utilization Histogram.description: STA load for connection statistics periods. Histogram bin range: 0 - 255 Histogram bin: 16 bins with a range step 16

Tx MPDUs in AMPDUs in Link Histogram.description: Accumulate counting of Tx Packets (MPDUs) in AMPDUs from start of connection per Link, Histogram: 0 - 15, 16 - 31 ... Jumps of 16 .... 992 - 1007, 1008 - 1023 MPDUs

Full BA Tx MPDUs in AMPDUs in Link Histo.description: Accumulate counting of Tx Packets (MPDUs) in AMPDUs from start of connection per Link, Histogram: 64 MPDUs, 256 MPDUs, 512 MPDUs, 1024 MPDUs

Rx MPDUs in AMPDUs in Link Histogram.description: Accumulate counting of Rx Packets (MPDUs)  in AMPDUs from start of connection per Link, Histogram: 0 - 15, 16 - 31 ... Jumps of 16 .... 992 - 1007, 1008 - 1023 MPDUs

Full BA Rx MPDUs in AMPDUs in Link Histo.description: Accumulate counting of Rx Packets (MPDUs) in AMPDUs from start of connection per Link, Histogram: 64 MPDUs, 256 MPDUs, 512 MPDUs, 1024 MPDUs

Tx RU Size in Link Histogram.description: Transmitted PPDU RU Size per Link from start of current connection. Histogram of 8 bins: 26 Tones, 52 Tones, 106 Tones, 242 Tones, 484 Tones, 996 Tones, 996x2 Tones, 996x4 Tones (the units are Strings representing number of Tones)

Channel Load Histogram.description: Channel Load Histogram per Link indicates the channel load percentage (Unknown can be a valid Values). Histogram includes 22 Bins : Unknown Bin + 21 Number bins. 1 bin represents 0 and 20 bin each represents 5% : [1, 5, 10, 15, …, 100] Conditions for customer for ignoring Channel Load Results : If ELP > ACT, In Wi-Fi-7 EMLSR mode, Channel Load Histogram is not updated.

Channel Load by US Histogram.description: Channel Load Histogram per Link indicates the channel load percentage of the STA. Histogram includes 21 bins. 1 bin represents 0 and 20 bin each represents 5%: [1, 5, 10, 15, ..., 100]. Conditions for customer for ignoring Channel Load Results: If ELP > ACT. In Wi-Fi7 EMLSR mode, Channel Load Histogram is not updated

Channel Load Active Total Time (ACT) Cou.description: Channel Load Active Total Time in Link in microseconds. In Wi-Fi7 EMLSR mode, ACT is set to 0. Unit: microseconds

Channel Load Elapsed Time (ELP) Counter.description: Channel Load Elapsed Time (ELP) Without Measurements in Link in microseconds. In Wi-Fi7 EMLSR mode, ELP is set to 0. Unit: microseconds

STA MAC Address.description: The MAC Address Identifier of the Local Wireless product. Typically representing the Self-MAC-Address (may be created dynamically, and the one that is actually used). Structured with 6 Octets (Bytes) values. e.g. 12:34:56:78:9A:BC

Is MLD AP.description: Indicates whether reported AP is a Multi-Link-Device (MLD, WiFi7) AP

AP MLD MAC Address.description: The AP side Multi-Link-Device, AP MLD MAC Address

SSID.description: The Current Wi-Fi SSID (Network Name) The Device Is Connected To

Connection Type.description: The type of the reported Connection (mainly used for Dual - BSS / Dual - Radio mode cases, and for the future 802.11be Multi - Link operations). Options: [Primary BSS, Secondary BSS, Primary MLD BSS, Secondary MLD BSS] Default : Primary BSS.

MPDU Frames Sum.description: SUM of Total Tx and Rx MGMT/Ctrl/Data MPDUs

MSDU Frames Sum.description: SUM of Total Tx and Rx Data MSDUs

Tx MPDU Frames Sum.description: SUM of Total Tx - Only MGMT/Ctrl/Data MPDUs

Rx MPDU Frames Sum.description: SUM of Total Rx - Only MGMT/Ctrl/Data MPDU

Tx Authentication Request and Reauthenti.description: Accumulated Counter of Tx Authentication Request Management Frames

Rx Authentication Response and Reauthent.description: Accumulated Counter of Rx Authentication Response Management Frames

Tx Association Request and Reassociation.description: Accumulated Counter of Tx Association Request and Tx Reassociation Request Management Frames

Rx Association Response and Reassociatio.description: Accumulated Counter of Rx Association Response and Rx Reassociation Response Management Frames

Rx De-Authentication Frames Counter.description: Accumulated Counter of Rx De-Authentication Management Frames

Rx Disassociation Frames Counter.description: Accumulated Counter of Rest of Rx Disassociation Management, it shall include Header Type Value = 00 (Management Frame) and SubType = 0x0a (for DisassociationDisassn)

Frame Latency per TID Histogram.description: TX Data Frames Latency counted per TID from the time the frame entered the MAC (Driver) till the time the frame is acknowledged. Unit: milliseconds

Connection Link ID.description: Identify the Links ID (for Multi-Links-Device, WiFi7 Case), Indicates the Link ID that is reported by MLD AP. In case of Legacy Mode (Non-multi-Links, i.e. Single-Link-Device), set to '0'.

BSSID.description: The BSSID of the AP as defined by 802.11 spec. In case of Multi-link AP (802.11be), it is the BSSID (i.e. the MAC Address) of a specific link within the MLD. Note: Should not be confused with the MLD MAC Address, which is shared by all links in MLD and is reported via a separate and dedicated Data-Point.

Band.description: The Current Band used by this Network (AP)

Channel.description: The Current Channel used by this Network (AP)

Operational Channel Width.description: The actual operational used Channel Width over the active link

Air Time in Link Sum.description: Total Rx and Tx Air Time from Start of the connection In microseconds per Link. Unit: microseconds

Tx Air Time in Link Counter.description: Total Tx Only Air Time from Start of the connection In microseconds per Link. Unit: microseconds

Rx Air Time in Link Counter.description: Total Rx Only Air Time from Start of the connection In microseconds per Link. Unit: microseconds

MPDUs Frames in Link Sum.description: Sum of MGMT/Ctrl/Data Frames for specific Link

MSDUs Frames in Link Sum.description: Sum of MSDUs Frames for specific Link

Tx MPDUs Frames in Link Counter.description: Sum of Tx Only MGMT/Ctrl/Data Frames for specific Link

Rx MPDUs Frames in Link Counter.description: Sum of RX Only In This Link MGMT/Ctrl/Data Frames for specific Link

MGMT Frames in Link Sum.description: Sum of Tx-Rx MGMT Frames for specific Link. Note: Doesn't include Probe Request frame

Latest RSSI A Beacon Counter.description: Antenna A, Beacon Frame based, Latest available RSSI in dBm (Non-Valid Default Value shall be: 0)

RSSI A Beacon Histogram.description: Antenna A, Beacon Frame based, Standard RSSI. Values are defined: 0 to -120 dBm Histogram bin value contains a counter of number of packets within the bin range. There will be a histogram for each BSSID (or MLD) of the connection it relates to. There will be a histogram for each chain. Non-Valid results or antenna not used (e.g. the packet had been received by one chain, then the data of the non-active chain shall not be included and it is marked with RSSI=0) shall be converted to 0.   Histogram of 36 bins: Bin for the range of -120dBm to -95dBm. Bin for every 1dB over the range of -94dBm to -75dBm. Bin for every 2dB over the range of -74dBm to -65dBm. Bin for every 4dB over the range of -64dBm to -45dBm. Bin for every 8dB over the range of -44dBm to -21dBm. Bin for the range of -20dBm to -1dBm. Bin for the value of 0 is for packets not received in this chain

Latest RSSI A Data Counter.description: Antenna A, Data Frame based, Latest available RSSI in dBm (Non Valid, Default Value shall be: 0)

BSSLoad IE Station Number Histogram.description: Based on Beacon and Probe-Response Legacy BSS Load IE for the specific BSS connection received by the driver during short measurement periods; Histogram 6 bin range: [1..5, 6..10, 11..15, 16..20, 21..30, Greater than 30]

BSSLoad IE Channel Utilization Histogram.description: Based on Beacon and Probe-Response Legacy BSS Load IE for the specific BSS connection received by the driver during short measurement periods; Histogram bin range: 0 - 255; Histogram bin : 16 bins with a range step 16

BSSLoad IE Available Admission Capacity.description: Based on Beacon and Probe-Response Legacy BSS Load IE for the specific BSS connection received by the driver during short measurement periods; Histogram bin range : 0 - 65535; Histogram bin : 16 bins with a range step 4096

CCA Indications During Tx in Link Counte.description: Number of CCA indicated when trying to transmit from start of current connection

Tx Attempts in Link Counter.description: Number of Tx Attempts (not Tx due CCA) from start of current connection

Tx Retries in Link Counter.description: Number of MPDUs retransmissions from the start of current connection

Tx MGMT Frames in Link Counter.description: Accumulated Counter of Tx Management Frames for Specific Link. Note: Accumulation doesnt include Probe Request and Probe Response frames

Rx MGMT Frames in Link Counter.description: Accumulated Counter of Rx Management Frames for specific Link. Note: Accumulation doesnt include Probe Request frame

Tx Action Frames in Link Counter.description: Accumulated Counter of Tx Action Management Frames for specific Link

Rx Action Frames in Link Counter.description: Accumulated Counter of Rx Action Management Frames for specific Link

Tx Data Frames in Link Counter.description: Accumulated Counter of Tx Data Frames for specific Link

Rx Data Frames in Link Counter.description: Accumulated Counter of Rx Data Frames for specific Link

Tx Packet-Loss in Link Counter.description: Number of dropped MPDUs which were not successfully transmitted from start of current connection

Tx Bytes Per TID in Link Counter.description: Sum of all Bytes of Data frames which were Tx per TID in specific Link. Note: in case there is different MAC it will be as different Link and therefore no need to add MAC Sum shall not include MPDU Headers and Security overheads. Counter counts in 64 Bytes resolution Note : Legacy frames which doesn't include TID are not counted. (where Legacy frames are CCK or OFDM)

Rx Bytes Per TID in Link Counter.description: Sum of all Bytes of Data frames which were Rx per TID on specific Link. Note: in case there is different MAC it will be as different Link and therefore no need to add MAC Sum shall not include MPDU Headers and Security overheads Counter counts in 64 Bytes resolution Note : Legacy frames which doesn't include TID are not counted. (where Legacy frames are CCK or OFDM)

Bad-CRCs in Link Counter.description: Number of received MPDUs with CRC error from start of current connection

Channel Load Active Total Time (ACT) Cou.description: Channel Load Active Total Time in Link in microseconds. In Wi-Fi7 EMLSR mode, ACT is set to 0. Unit: microseconds

EVM Beacon Histogram.description: Received Beacon frames EVM Values histogram Histogram bin value contains a counter of number of packets within the bin range There will be a histogram for each BSSID (or MLD) Link of the connection it relates to. If the packet had been received by one chain, then the data of the non - active chain should not be included. Beacon is always 1 spatial stream Histogram of 42 bins: Bin for every 1dB over the range of 0dB to 39dB. Bin for the range of 40dB to 50dB. Bin for -1 is dedicated for debug purposes.

EVM Data 1SS Histogram.description: Received Data frames with 1 Spatial Stream EVM Values histogram Histogram bin value contains a counter of number of packets within the bin range There will be a histogram for each BSSID (or MLD) Link of the connection it relates to Histogram of 42 bins: Bin for every 1dB over the range of 0dB to 39dB.Bin for the range of 40dB to 50dB. Bin for -1 is dedicated for debug purposes.

EVM Data 2SS Histogram.description: Received Data frames with 2 Spatial Stream minimum EVM per stream Values histogram. Histogram bin value contains a counter of number of packets within the bin range There will be a histogram for each BSSID (or MLD) Link of the connection it relates to The EVM value is the minimum between the EVM of spatial stream 1 and spatial stream 2 Histogram of 42 bins: Bin for every 1dB over the range of - 1dB to 39dB. Bin for the range of 40dB to 50dB. Bin for -1 is dedicated for debug purposes.

EVM Data 2SS Delta Histogram.description: Received Data frames with 2 Spatial Stream absolute delta between the EVM of each of it's streams values histogram Histogram bin value contains a counter of number of packets within the bin range There will be a histogram for each BSSID (or MLD) Link of the connection it relates to Histogram of 18 bins, as follows : Bin for every 1dB over the range of 0dB to 8dB. Bin for every 4dB over the range of 9dB to 32dB. Bin for the range of 33dB to 40dB. Bin for the range of 41dB to 50dB. Bin for -1 is dedicated for debug purposes.

Disconnected Antenna.description: The Disconnected Antenna. A string with value “A” or “B”

Disconnection Reason.description: In case of disconnection from AP, indicates the disconnection reason: Internal Termination Disconnect Initiated by OS Task Roam from OS - missing Disconnect Initiated by Roam No Candidate Disconnect Initiated by Roaming Disconnect Initiated by AP
enum: Disconnect Initiated by Roam No Candidate

Disconnection Frame.description: The Raw Disassociation or Deauthentication Frame (if Exists, meaning if sent by AP)

Disconnection Frame Timestamp.description: Internal Timestamp of the Disconnection or Deauthentication Frames

Connection Duration.description: The Time in milliseconds passed from Connection establishment to the reported disconnection. Unit: milliseconds

STA MAC Address.description: The MAC Address Identifier of the Local Wireless product. Typically representing the Self-MAC-Address (may be created dynamically, and the one that is actually used). Structured with 6 Octets (Bytes) values. e.g. 12:34:56:78:9A:BC

Is MLD AP.description: Indicates whether reported AP is a Multi-Link-Device (MLD, WiFi7) AP

AP MLD MAC Address.description: The AP side Multi-Link-Device, AP MLD MAC Address

Connection Type.description: The type of the reported Connection (mainly used for Dual - BSS / Dual - Radio mode cases, and for the future 802.11be Multi - Link operations). Options: [Primary BSS, Secondary BSS, Primary MLD BSS, Secondary MLD BSS] Default : Primary BSS.

SSID.description: The Current Wi-Fi SSID (Network Name) The Device Is Connected To

Windows OS Link Quality.description: The Wi-Fi Signal Strength Indication from the OS Level

Roaming Reason.description: Roaming Reason. Relevant only when 'Connection Trigger' is 'Roaming'. Roaming Reason values : -Not Roaming - Missed Beacons - Low RSSI - Exclude Current AP from OS - Better AP Found - BT activity on current band /* most of this value shall replaced wit the BT activity from the values below, hence most of the cases are not on current band */ - 11v recommendation - 11v force - CSA with long Quiet - Mode - Current AP’s properties change - Critical AP’s properties change (require reconnection to same AP) - Long CCA (require reconnection to same AP with 20MHz BW) - OS Initiate Roaming – Recommendation - OS Initiate Roaming – Force - BT Activity /* this shall replace most of the BT activity on current band from the values above */ - Other

Connection Link ID.description: Identify the Links ID (for Multi-Links-Device, WiFi7 Case), Indicates the Link ID that is reported by MLD AP. In case of Legacy Mode (Non-multi-Links, i.e. Single-Link-Device), set to '0'.

BSSID.description: The BSSID of the AP as defined by 802.11 spec. In case of Multi-link AP (802.11be), it is the BSSID (i.e. the MAC Address) of a specific link within the MLD. Note: Should not be confused with the MLD MAC Address, which is shared by all links in MLD and is reported via a separate and dedicated Data-Point.

Probe Response Frame.description: The latest raw Probe-Response of reported AP

Datapoint of the MAC Address Identifier of the Local Wireless product representing usually the Self MAC Address (may be created dynamically, and the one that is actually used).

EMLSR Exit Reasons.description: EMLSR Exit Reasons are list of strings that represents all the conditions which can cause to exiting from EMLSR.  At specific time, there can be more than one exit cause. EMLSR Exit Reasons : 0 - low RSSI 1 - Missed Beacons on Primary Link  2 - Missed Beacon on Secondary Link  3 - CSA  4 - MVM P2P  5 - LowTpT  6 - Low Number of MPDUs in Link  7 - Low Number of A - MPDU in Link  8 - D3  9 - Reset  10 - FW Recommendation  11 - Link Removed  12 - Disconnection  13 - DPTF request  14 - Low Channel Load in EMLSR  15 - BT Restriction  31 - Not Defined

enum: SoftAP, GO, P2P Client, P2P Device were activated

Link Information Type.description: Link Information Type indicates in MLD Connection: -Primary Link - if the link is Primary Link or -Secondary Link - if the link is Secondary Link The Link Type can be updated during MLD connection.

EMLSR AMPDUs in Link.description: EMLSR Counters which count the Number of AMPDUs in Link in TX and RX Exit criteria.  TX AMPDUs Percentage in Link - Percentage of number of TX AMPDUs in link compared to the total number of TX AMPDUs over the two EMLSR links.  Number of TX AMPDUs Link at T - Number of TX AMPDUs counted on this link at time T. RX AMPDUs Percentage in Link - Percentage of number of RX AMPDUs in link compared to the total number of RX AMPDUs over the two EMLSR links.  Number of RX AMPDUs Link at T - Number of RX AMPDUs counted on this link at time T.

enum: Number of TX AMPDUs Link at T
Number of TX AMPDUs Link at T-1
Number of TX AMPDUs Link at T-2
Number of RX AMPDUs Link at T

EMLSR Low Number of MPDUs in Link.description: EMLSR Counters which count the Number of MPDUs in Link in TX and RX Exit criteria. TX MPDUs Percentage in Link - Percentage of number of TX MPDUs in link compared to the total number of TX MPDUs over the two EMLSR links.Number of TX MPDUs Link at T - Number of TX MPDUs counted on this link at time T. RX MPDUs Percentage in Link - Percentage of number of RX MPDUs in link compared to the total number of RX MPDUs over the two EMLSR links. Number of RX MPDUs Link at T - Number of RX MPDUs counted on this link at time T.

enum: Number of TX MPDUs Link at T

Operational Authentication Algorithm.description: The actual Authentication mode that was used when associated to AP, based on the selected AKM Suite Type from the RSNE element

Authentication Request Timestamp.description: Timestamp of the Authentication Request frame. Refers to the first Authentication Request Frame (in case there were multiple retries or multiple frames)

Authentication Request Retries Counter.description: Number of Retries of the Authentication Request frame during last Association cycle

Authentication Request Frame.description: Raw Authentication Request Frame. Refers to first Authentication Request Frame during last Association cycle

Authentication Response Timestamp.description: Timestamp of the Authentication Response frame. Refers to the last Authentication Response frame (in case there were multiple frames)

Authentication Response Frame.description: Raw Authentication Response Frame. Refers to last Authentication Response Frame during last Association cycle

Association Request Timestamp.description: Timestamp of the Association Request frame. Refers to the first Association Request frame (in case there were multiple retries or multiple frames)

Association Request Retries Counter.description: Number of Retries of the Association Request frame during last Association cycle

Association Request Frame.description: Raw Association Request Frame (or Reassociation). Refers to first Association Request Frame during last Association cycle.

Association Response Timestamp.description: Timestamp of the Association Response frame. Refers to the last Association Response frame (in case there were multiple frames)

Association Response Frame.description: Raw Association Response Frame. Refers to last Association Response Frame during last Association cycle

Connection Link ID.description: Identify the Links ID (for Multi-Links-Device, WiFi7 Case), Indicates the Link ID that is reported by MLD AP. In case of Legacy Mode (Non-multi-Links, i.e. Single-Link-Device), set to '0'.

BSSID.description: The BSSID of the AP as defined by 802.11 spec. In case of Multi-link AP (802.11be), it is the BSSID (i.e. the MAC Address) of a specific link within the MLD. Note: Should not be confused with the MLD MAC Address, which is shared by all links in MLD and is reported via a separate and dedicated Data-Point.

Spatial Streams Tx.description: Max number of Spatial Streams that are mutually supported by AP and Client on Tx

Band.description: The Current Band used by this Network (AP)

Channel.description: The Current Channel used by this Network (AP)

Operational Channel Width.description: The actual operational used Channel Width over the active link

Radio Type.description: In case DP is reported with 'connection' context - The agreed Wi-Fi generation as part of the association process, based on the the Legacy/HT/VHT/HE/EHT/UHR capabilities that are reported in AP's Association-Response. However, in case the report is following connection failure prior to association completion, the report will be based on the Max - Common Wi-Fi Generation between our Device and AP(as reported for 'scan' context). In case DP is reported with 'scan' context - Max(most advanced) supported Wi-Fi generation of the AP, based on the the Legacy / HT / VHT / HE / EHT / UHR capabilities that are reported in AP's Beacon/Probe-Response. The following is the Spec - Name vs Wi-Fi Generations Mapping: Legacy - Wi-Fi3, HT - Wi-Fi4, VHT - Wi-Fi5, HE - Wi-Fi6, EHT - Wi-Fi7, UHR - Wi-Fi8

Multicast Cipher Type.description: The Multicast Cipher that is used by AP. The enumeration of the Multicast Cipher type is the Cipher - Suite Number that is reported by RSNE :0 - No Cipher Algorithm(Note : This is the only exception from the Cipher - Suite Number definition) 1 - WEP40 2 - TKIP 4 - CCMP128 5 - WEP104 8 - GCMP128 9 - GCMP256

Unicast Cipher Type.description: The Unicast Cipher type (Per Link), One of [No Cipher Algorithm, WEP40, TKIP, CCMP, WEP104, BIP, GCMP 128, GCMP 256, BIP GMAC 128, BIP CMAC 256, WPA USE Group, RSN USE Group, WEP, IHV Start, MFP CCMP, MKP TKIP, CKIP, Generic]

AP Radio PMF Indication.description: The AP PMF support indication. Boolean value: False - 0 - PMF is not supported (RSNE's MFPC=MFPR=0) True - 1 - AP is PMF Capable or Required (RSNE's MFPC=1 Or MFPR=1)

Beacon Frame.description: The latest raw Beacon of reported AP

Probe Response Frame.description: The latest raw Probe-Response of reported AP

Invalid IEs in the Beacon Frame.description: List of Invalid elements that were reported by AP/Link in the Beacon Frame

Invalid IEs in the Probe Response Frame.description: List of Invalid elements that were reported by AP/Link in the Probe Response Frame

required: Invalid IEs in the Beacon Frame
Invalid IEs in the Probe Response Frame

Reported Connection Failure Reason.description: In case of connection establishment failure, indicates the failure reason: Internal Termination (e.g. other reasons - not any of the following reasons) Abort from OS Deauth from AP Disassoc from AP 80211 Assoc Failure 80211 Auth Failure Invalid Capabilities Tx/Rx Failure. This is a More updated Datapoint vs the original Connection Failure Reason

Disconnection Frame.description: The Raw Disassociation or Deauthentication Frame (if Exists, meaning if sent by AP)

Disconnection Frame Timestamp.description: Internal timestamp of the Disconnection or Deauthentication Frames

STA MAC Address.description: The MAC Address Identifier of the Local Wireless product. Typically representing the Self-MAC-Address (may be created dynamically, and the one that is actually used). Structured with 6 Octets (Bytes) values. e.g. 12:34:56:78:9A:BC

Is MLD AP.description: Indicates whether reported AP is a Multi-Link-Device (MLD, WiFi7) AP

AP MLD MAC Address.description: The AP side Multi-Link-Device, AP MLD MAC Address

Connection Type.description: The type of the reported Connection (mainly used for Dual - BSS / Dual - Radio mode cases, and for the future 802.11be Multi - Link operations). Options: [Primary BSS, Secondary BSS, Primary MLD BSS, Secondary MLD BSS] Default : Primary BSS.

SSID.description: The Current Wi-Fi SSID (Network Name) The Device Is Connected To

AP Manufacturer Name.description: The Name of the AP Manufacturer: e.g., Netgear

AP Model Name.description: The Name of the AP Model: e.g., R7000

Windows OS Link Quality.description: The Wi-Fi Signal Strength Indication from the OS Level

Connection Trigger Reason.description: Reason that triggered the reported Connection. Options: [ New connection, Roaming]

Roaming Reason.description: Roaming Reason. Relevant only when 'Connection Trigger' is 'Roaming'. Roaming Reason values : -Not Roaming - Missed Beacons - Low RSSI - Exclude Current AP from OS - Better AP Found - BT activity on current band /* most of this value shall replaced wit the BT activity from the values below, hence most of the cases are not on current band */ - 11v recommendation - 11v force - CSA with long Quiet - Mode - Current AP’s properties change - Critical AP’s properties change (require reconnection to same AP) - Long CCA (require reconnection to same AP with 20MHz BW) - OS Initiate Roaming – Recommendation - OS Initiate Roaming – Force - BT Activity /* this shall replace most of the BT activity on current band from the values above */ - Other

Operational Authentication Algorithm AKM.description: The actual Authentication mode that was used when associated to AP. The actual Authentication mode is a string based on the Authentication mode Name that is based on the AKM - Suite - Type Number that is reported by RSNE : 0 - Open mode, 1 - Enterprise 802.1X authentication with SHA1, 2 - Personal PSK authentication with SHA1, 3 - Enterprise 802.1X FT authentication with SHA256, 4 - Personal PSK FT authentication with SHA256, 5 - Enterprise 802.1X authentication with SHA256, 6 - Personal PSK authentication with SHA256, 7 - TDLS, 8 - Personal SAE authentication with SHA256, 9 - Personal SAE FT authentication with SHA256, 11 - Enterprise 802.1X authentication with SHA256 - GCMP - 128, 12 - Enterprise 802.1X authentication with SHA256 - GCMP - 256 CNSA, 13 - Enterprise 802.1X FT authentication with SHA256 - CCMP - 256 & GCMP - 256 CNSA, 18 - OWE authentication with SHA256, 22 - Enterprise 802.1X FT authentication with SHA384, 23 - Enterprise 802.1X authentication with SHA384, 24 - Personal SAE authentication with SHA384 / 256 based on selected DH group, 25 - Personal SAE FT authentication with SHA384 / 256 based on selected DH group, 99 - Not Defined
enum: Open mode (reported when there is no RSNE element)
Enterprise 802.1X FT (Fast BSS Transition) authentication with SHA256
Personal PSK FT (Fast BSS Transition) authentication with SHA256
Personal SAE FT (Fast BSS Transition) authentication with SHA256
GCMP-256 CNSA: Enterprise 802.1X authentication with SHA256
CCMP-256 & GCMP-256 CNSA: Enterprise 802.1X FT (Fast BSS Transition) authentication with SHA256
Enterprise 802.1X FT (Fast BSS Transition) authentication with SHA384
Personal SAE authentication with SHA384 / 256 based on selected DH group
Personal SAE FT (Fast BSS Transition) authentication with SHA384 / 256 based on selected DH group

Original Authentication Algorithm AKM Su.description: The original Authentication Algorithm used during first successful connection to AP from current session. (this is reported in Wi-Fi Connection Info / Wi-Fi Successful connection events). Note that in some cases, such as 'Fast Roaming', the Operational Authentication Algorithm may be different than the Original Authentication Algorithm.
enum: Open mode (reported when there is no RSNE element)
Enterprise 802.1X FT (Fast BSS Transition) authentication with SHA256
Personal PSK FT (Fast BSS Transition) authentication with SHA256
Personal SAE FT (Fast BSS Transition) authentication with SHA256
GCMP-256 CNSA: Enterprise 802.1X authentication with SHA256
CCMP-256 & GCMP-256 CNSA: Enterprise 802.1X FT (Fast BSS Transition) authentication with SHA256
Enterprise 802.1X FT (Fast BSS Transition) authentication with SHA384
Personal SAE authentication with SHA384 / 256 based on selected DH group
Personal SAE FT (Fast BSS Transition) authentication with SHA384 / 256 based on selected DH group

Authentication Response Timestamp.description: Timestamp of the Authentication Response frame. Refers to the last Authentication Response frame (in case there were multiple frames)

Association Request Timestamp.description: Timestamp of the Association Request frame. Refers to the first Association Request frame (in case there were multiple retries or multiple frames)

Connection Link ID.description: Identify the Links ID (for Multi-Links-Device, WiFi7 Case), Indicates the Link ID that is reported by MLD AP. In case of Legacy Mode (Non-multi-Links, i.e. Single-Link-Device), set to '0'.

BSSID.description: The BSSID of the AP as defined by 802.11 spec. In case of Multi-link AP (802.11be), it is the BSSID (i.e. the MAC Address) of a specific link within the MLD. Note: Should not be confused with the MLD MAC Address, which is shared by all links in MLD and is reported via a separate and dedicated Data-Point.

Spatial Streams Tx.description: Max number of Spatial Streams that are mutually supported by AP and Client on Tx

Band.description: The Current Band used by this Network (AP)

Channel.description: The Current Channel used by this Network (AP)

Operational Channel Width.description: The actual operational used Channel Width over the active link

Radio Type.description: In case DP is reported with 'connection' context - The agreed Wi-Fi generation as part of the association process, based on the the Legacy/HT/VHT/HE/EHT/UHR capabilities that are reported in AP's Association-Response. However, in case the report is following connection failure prior to association completion, the report will be based on the Max - Common Wi-Fi Generation between our Device and AP(as reported for 'scan' context). In case DP is reported with 'scan' context - Max(most advanced) supported Wi-Fi generation of the AP, based on the the Legacy / HT / VHT / HE / EHT / UHR capabilities that are reported in AP's Beacon/Probe-Response. The following is the Spec - Name vs Wi-Fi Generations Mapping: Legacy - Wi-Fi3, HT - Wi-Fi4, VHT - Wi-Fi5, HE - Wi-Fi6, EHT - Wi-Fi7, UHR - Wi-Fi8

Multicast Cipher.description: The Multicast Cipher that is used by AP. The Multicast Cipher Suite is a string based on the Cipher-Suite Name that is based on the Cipher-Suite Number that is reported by RSNE: 0 - No Cipher Algorithm (Note: This is the only exception from the Cipher-Suite Number definition) 1- WEP40 2 - TKIP 4 - CCMP128 5 - WEP104 8 - GCMP128 9 - GCMP256

Multicast Cipher Type.description: The Multicast Cipher that is used by AP. The enumeration of the Multicast Cipher type is the Cipher - Suite Number that is reported by RSNE :0 - No Cipher Algorithm(Note : This is the only exception from the Cipher - Suite Number definition) 1 - WEP40 2 - TKIP 4 - CCMP128 5 - WEP104 8 - GCMP128 9 - GCMP256

Unicast Cipher.description: The actual Unicast Cipher type (Per Link) that is selected for the associated AP. The Unicast Cipher Suite is a string based on the Cipher-Suite Name that is based on the Cipher-Suite Number that is reported by RSNE: 0 - No Cipher Algorithm (Note: This is an exception from the Cipher-Suite Number definition) 1- WEP40 2 - TKIP 4 - CCMP128 5 - WEP104 8 - GCMP128 9 - GCMP256

Unicast Cipher Type.description: The Unicast Cipher type (Per Link), One of [No Cipher Algorithm, WEP40, TKIP, CCMP, WEP104, BIP, GCMP 128, GCMP 256, BIP GMAC 128, BIP CMAC 256, WPA USE Group, RSN USE Group, WEP, IHV Start, MFP CCMP, MKP TKIP, CKIP, Generic]

AP Radio PMF Indication.description: The AP PMF support indication. Boolean value: False - 0 - PMF is not supported (RSNE's MFPC=MFPR=0) True - 1 - AP is PMF Capable or Required (RSNE's MFPC=1 Or MFPR=1)

BSSID.description: The BSSID of the AP as defined by 802.11 spec. In case of Multi-link AP (802.11be), it is the BSSID (i.e. the MAC Address) of a specific link within the MLD. Note: Should not be confused with the MLD MAC Address, which is shared by all links in MLD and is reported via a separate and dedicated Data-Point.

Spatial Streams Tx.description: Max number of Spatial Streams that are mutually supported by AP and Client on Tx

Band.description: The Current Band used by this Network (AP)

Channel.description: The Current Channel used by this Network (AP)

Operational Channel Width.description: The actual operational used Channel Width over the active link

Radio Type.description: In case DP is reported with 'connection' context - The agreed Wi-Fi generation as part of the association process, based on the the Legacy/HT/VHT/HE/EHT/UHR capabilities that are reported in AP's Association-Response. However, in case the report is following connection failure prior to association completion, the report will be based on the Max - Common Wi-Fi Generation between our Device and AP(as reported for 'scan' context). In case DP is reported with 'scan' context - Max(most advanced) supported Wi-Fi generation of the AP, based on the the Legacy / HT / VHT / HE / EHT / UHR capabilities that are reported in AP's Beacon/Probe-Response. The following is the Spec - Name vs Wi-Fi Generations Mapping: Legacy - Wi-Fi3, HT - Wi-Fi4, VHT - Wi-Fi5, HE - Wi-Fi6, EHT - Wi-Fi7, UHR - Wi-Fi8

Multicast Cipher Type.description: The Multicast Cipher that is used by AP. The enumeration of the Multicast Cipher type is the Cipher - Suite Number that is reported by RSNE :0 - No Cipher Algorithm(Note : This is the only exception from the Cipher - Suite Number definition) 1 - WEP40 2 - TKIP 4 - CCMP128 5 - WEP104 8 - GCMP128 9 - GCMP256

Unicast Cipher Type.description: The Unicast Cipher type (Per Link), One of [No Cipher Algorithm, WEP40, TKIP, CCMP, WEP104, BIP, GCMP 128, GCMP 256, BIP GMAC 128, BIP CMAC 256, WPA USE Group, RSN USE Group, WEP, IHV Start, MFP CCMP, MKP TKIP, CKIP, Generic]

AP Radio PMF Indication.description: The AP PMF support indication. Boolean value: False - 0 - PMF is not supported (RSNE's MFPC=MFPR=0) True - 1 - AP is PMF Capable or Required (RSNE's MFPC=1 Or MFPR=1)

Beacon Frame.description: The latest raw Beacon of reported AP

Probe Response Frame.description: The latest raw Probe-Response of reported AP

STA MAC Address.description: The MAC Address Identifier of the Local Wireless product. Typically representing the Self-MAC-Address (may be created dynamically, and the one that is actually used). Structured with 6 Octets (Bytes) values. e.g. 12:34:56:78:9A:BC

Is MLD AP.description: Indicates whether reported AP is a Multi-Link-Device (MLD, WiFi7) AP

AP MLD MAC Address.description: The AP side Multi-Link-Device, AP MLD MAC Address

Connection Type.description: The type of the reported Connection (mainly used for Dual - BSS / Dual - Radio mode cases, and for the future 802.11be Multi - Link operations). Options: [Primary BSS, Secondary BSS, Primary MLD BSS, Secondary MLD BSS] Default : Primary BSS.

SSID.description: The Current Wi-Fi SSID (Network Name) The Device Is Connected To

AP Manufacturer Name.description: The Name of the AP Manufacturer: e.g., Netgear

AP Model Name.description: The Name of the AP Model: e.g., R7000

Windows OS Link Quality.description: The Wi-Fi Signal Strength Indication from the OS Level

Connection Trigger Reason.description: Reason that triggered the reported Connection. Options: [ New connection, Roaming]

Roaming Reason.description: Roaming Reason. Relevant only when 'Connection Trigger' is 'Roaming'. Roaming Reason values : -Not Roaming - Missed Beacons - Low RSSI - Exclude Current AP from OS - Better AP Found - BT activity on current band /* most of this value shall replaced wit the BT activity from the values below, hence most of the cases are not on current band */ - 11v recommendation - 11v force - CSA with long Quiet - Mode - Current AP’s properties change - Critical AP’s properties change (require reconnection to same AP) - Long CCA (require reconnection to same AP with 20MHz BW) - OS Initiate Roaming – Recommendation - OS Initiate Roaming – Force - BT Activity /* this shall replace most of the BT activity on current band from the values above */ - Other

Operational Authentication Algorithm AKM.description: The actual Authentication mode that was used when associated to AP. The actual Authentication mode is a string based on the Authentication mode Name that is based on the AKM - Suite - Type Number that is reported by RSNE : 0 - Open mode, 1 - Enterprise 802.1X authentication with SHA1, 2 - Personal PSK authentication with SHA1, 3 - Enterprise 802.1X FT authentication with SHA256, 4 - Personal PSK FT authentication with SHA256, 5 - Enterprise 802.1X authentication with SHA256, 6 - Personal PSK authentication with SHA256, 7 - TDLS, 8 - Personal SAE authentication with SHA256, 9 - Personal SAE FT authentication with SHA256, 11 - Enterprise 802.1X authentication with SHA256 - GCMP - 128, 12 - Enterprise 802.1X authentication with SHA256 - GCMP - 256 CNSA, 13 - Enterprise 802.1X FT authentication with SHA256 - CCMP - 256 & GCMP - 256 CNSA, 18 - OWE authentication with SHA256, 22 - Enterprise 802.1X FT authentication with SHA384, 23 - Enterprise 802.1X authentication with SHA384, 24 - Personal SAE authentication with SHA384 / 256 based on selected DH group, 25 - Personal SAE FT authentication with SHA384 / 256 based on selected DH group, 99 - Not Defined
enum: Open mode (reported when there is no RSNE element)
Enterprise 802.1X FT (Fast BSS Transition) authentication with SHA256
Personal PSK FT (Fast BSS Transition) authentication with SHA256
Personal SAE FT (Fast BSS Transition) authentication with SHA256
GCMP-256 CNSA: Enterprise 802.1X authentication with SHA256
CCMP-256 & GCMP-256 CNSA: Enterprise 802.1X FT (Fast BSS Transition) authentication with SHA256
Enterprise 802.1X FT (Fast BSS Transition) authentication with SHA384
Personal SAE authentication with SHA384 / 256 based on selected DH group
Personal SAE FT (Fast BSS Transition) authentication with SHA384 / 256 based on selected DH group

Original Authentication Algorithm AKM Su.description: The original Authentication Algorithm used during first successful connection to AP from current session. (this is reported in Wi-Fi Connection Info / Wi-Fi Successful connection events). Note that in some cases, such as 'Fast Roaming', the Operational Authentication Algorithm may be different than the Original Authentication Algorithm.
enum: Open mode (reported when there is no RSNE element)
Enterprise 802.1X FT (Fast BSS Transition) authentication with SHA256
Personal PSK FT (Fast BSS Transition) authentication with SHA256
Personal SAE FT (Fast BSS Transition) authentication with SHA256
GCMP-256 CNSA: Enterprise 802.1X authentication with SHA256
CCMP-256 & GCMP-256 CNSA: Enterprise 802.1X FT (Fast BSS Transition) authentication with SHA256
Enterprise 802.1X FT (Fast BSS Transition) authentication with SHA384
Personal SAE authentication with SHA384 / 256 based on selected DH group
Personal SAE FT (Fast BSS Transition) authentication with SHA384 / 256 based on selected DH group

Authentication Request Timestamp.description: Timestamp of the Authentication Request frame. Refers to the first Authentication Request Frame (in case there were multiple retries or multiple frames)

Authentication Request Retries Counter.description: Number of Retries of the Authentication Request frame during last Association cycle

Authentication Request Frame.description: Raw Authentication Request Frame. Refers to first Authentication Request Frame during last Association cycle

Authentication Response Timestamp.description: Timestamp of the Authentication Response frame. Refers to the last Authentication Response frame (in case there were multiple frames)

Authentication Response Frame.description: Raw Authentication Response Frame. Refers to last Authentication Response Frame during last Association cycle

Association Request Timestamp.description: Timestamp of the Association Request frame. Refers to the first Association Request frame (in case there were multiple retries or multiple frames)

Association Request Retries Counter.description: Number of Retries of the Association Request frame during last Association cycle

Association Request Frame.description: Raw Association Request Frame (or Reassociation). Refers to first Association Request Frame during last Association cycle.

Association Response Timestamp.description: Timestamp of the Association Response frame. Refers to the last Association Response frame (in case there were multiple frames)

Association Response Frame.description: Raw Association Response Frame. Refers to last Association Response Frame during last Association cycle

Connection Link ID.description: Identify the Links ID (for Multi-Links-Device, WiFi7 Case), Indicates the Link ID that is reported by MLD AP. In case of Legacy Mode (Non-multi-Links, i.e. Single-Link-Device), set to '0'.

Note: Should not be confused with the MLD MAC Address, which is shared by all links in MLD and is reported via a separate and dedicated Data-Point.

Spatial Streams Tx.description: Max number of Spatial Streams that are mutually supported by AP and Client on Tx

Band.description: The Current Band used by this Network (AP)

Channel.description: The Current Channel used by this Network (AP)

Operational Channel Width.description: The actual operational used Channel Width over the active link
